title: Air-fryer pork belly slices
subtitle: Crispy fat layers and tender meat in 20 minutes
excerpt: Pork belly slices cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 20 minutes. The high circulating heat renders the fat layers and crisps the skin surface without any deep frying. Dry the slices thoroughly before seasoning: surface moisture is the single biggest obstacle to crisp pork belly.
sourceNotes: Pork belly slices became one of the best-performing air-fryer recipes because the appliance directly addresses the main difficulty of pork belly: rendering the fat layers and crisping the skin without a long oven roast or deep fryer. The circulating air produces results that otherwise require either a very long low roast (3 to 4 hours) or a professional kitchen blow torch.

content.text: Pat the pork belly slices extremely dry before seasoning. Pork belly contains more moisture than most cuts, and any wetness on the surface will steam the skin rather than crisp it. Use several pieces of kitchen paper and press firmly: the surface should feel dry to the touch, not just less wet.

Pat the {{pork-belly}} slices thoroughly dry with kitchen paper. Rub on all sides with the {{smoked-paprika}}, {{garlic-powder}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Arrange the slices in a single layer (they can touch slightly but should not overlap). Cook at 200°C for 20 minutes, turning once at the 10-minute mark, until the skin surface is deep golden and the fat layers are rendered and glossy. Rest for 2 minutes. Drain on kitchen paper before serving if the rendered fat is excessive.

items.fix: Pat dry aggressively: moisture is the enemy of crisp pork skin. Slices thicker than 1.5 cm will not crisp in the same time. Score the skin side with a sharp knife before seasoning to help render the fat
items.cause: Pork belly slices not dried thoroughly enough, or too thick
items.symptom: Skin surface still soft and chewy rather than crispy

items.fix: A small amount of smoke is normal with fatty cuts. Place a slice of bread or a small amount of water in the drip tray to catch drips. Clean the air fryer after use
items.cause: Rendered fat dripping onto the heating element

items.fix: Increase to 210°C for the last 3 minutes. The fat needs high heat to fully render. If the air fryer only goes to 200°C, cook for 5 additional minutes on the fat-side-up position
items.cause: Not enough time, or temperature not high enough
items.symptom: Meat tender but fat layers not rendered

content.text: Air-fryer pork belly slices are a weeknight shortcut to a result that usually requires a 3-hour roast. Serve with apple sauce, egg fried rice, or steamed greens. A small amount of soy sauce and honey brushed on in the last 2 minutes makes a quick Asian-style glaze.
